focus: Adversarially refute-or-confirm F-002 (EPERM-before-heartbeat reclaim ordering) and F-004 (reclaim durability fsync) in bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs over range a9e9bdb0a5^..HEAD; read full acquire/classify/reclaim/heartbeat/respawn sequence.
noveltyJustification: Adversarial-verify pass: zero NEW findings. Re-adjudicated two carried-forward findings — F-002 downgraded P1->P2 (structurally confirmed, severity refuted by changelog-006-009 design intent + ESRCH-not-EPERM-on-dead + same-user multi-sandbox deployment), F-004 confirmed-at-P2. Both are pre-existing IDs being re-weighted, not new discoveries; weightedNew=0 -> ratio 0.0. Net effect on convergence: removes one P1 from the open set.

verdicts.evidence: mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:339 (unknown-eperm early return precedes staleness gate :346-348), :361 (bucketed with live-owner), :643-644 (reapOwnerBeforeRespawn ALSO refuses EPERM -> both reclaim routes blocked); BUT model-server-supervision.cjs:281 (EPERM->unknown-eperm only), launcher-lease.vitest.ts:360-367 (dead PID = ESRCH = reclaimable, no EPERM test), changelog-006-009:27 (EPERM=live cross-sandbox owner, RCA-confirmed correct semantic) => stuck trace needs cross-uid PID reuse, contradicting documented same-user multi-sandbox deployment; common EPERM case correct-by-design. Severity P1->P2.

mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:281-285 (writeOwnerLeaseFile renames without fsync) vs :294 (writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive fsyncs); reclaim caller :382, heartbeat caller :399. Asymmetry real. Impact bounded P2: renameSync atomic (no torn lease), total loss reopens clean no-owner state (re-acquirable :369), re-read :383-384 serializes racers, heartbeat self-heals every ttl/2=30s (:420-421). Confirmed P2.

findingDetails.title: EPERM owner lease bypasses both reclaim routes (heartbeat-staleness + dead-socket respawn) — real but narrow edge-case hardening gap, downgraded P1->P2
findingDetails.evidence: classifyOwnerLease returns 'unknown-eperm' at :339 BEFORE the 2xTTL heartbeat-staleness gate at :346-348; acquireOwnerLeaseFile buckets 'unknown-eperm' with 'live-owner' at :361 ({acquired:false}); reapOwnerBeforeRespawn independently refuses EPERM at :643-644 -> both documented reclaim escapes unreachable for an EPERM-resolving owner PID. processLiveness:281 maps EPERM->unknown-eperm only. Dead PIDs yield ESRCH->'stale-pid' (reclaimable, tested launcher-lease.vitest.ts:360-367), not EPERM. changelog-006-009 documents EPERM-as-live-lease as RCA-confirmed correct cross-sandbox semantic.
findingDetails.recommendation: Let an EPERM lease age out via a longer EPERM-specific TTL on the heartbeat gate, OR document the cross-uid PID-reuse corner as accepted-risk; add the EPERM-mock vitest the changelog lists as a follow-up. Not a release blocker for same-user multi-sandbox deployment.
findingDetails.scopeProof: all cited lines in-range (3419e0a3e9 added classification ordering; git log -S 'stale-heartbeat-reclaim'); EPERM branch + parity rationale in in-range changelog-006-009; working file == reviewed HEAD blob (git diff 12de3d3a7e empty).
findingDetails.claim: An owner lease whose ownerPid resolves to EPERM bypasses both reclaim routes; structurally confirmed but severity P1->P2 because the only stuck trace needs cross-uid PID reuse outside the documented same-user multi-sandbox deployment and the common EPERM case is correct-by-design.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Re-read BOTH reclaim routes end-to-end (heartbeat gate unreachable :339<:348; respawn route refuses :643-644 -> double-block real). Then sought deployment/intent counterevidence: changelog-006-009 = EPERM-as-live correct cross-sandbox semantic; dead PIDs = ESRCH (reclaimable, tested); EPERM-on-dead needs foreign-uid PID reuse outside documented model. Narrow edge survives; P1 does not.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: EPERM owners are intentionally treated as live because in the documented deployment they ARE live (different sandbox, same user); the staleness gate covers ESRCH-yielding dead/PID-reused owners. Only the foreign-uid PID-reuse corner escapes both routes -> real but low-likelihood, P2.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Already triggered (same-user multi-sandbox deployment satisfies original P2 condition). Re-escalate to P1 only if a multi-uid owner (root daemon vs user launcher, container-uid remap) is introduced for spec-memory.

findingDetails.title: Reclaim/heartbeat owner-lease write skips fsync, unlike the exclusive fresh-acquire writer — durability asymmetry confirmed at P2
findingDetails.evidence: writeOwnerLeaseFile (:281-285) writes tmp + renameSync with NO fsync, used by reclaim (:382) and heartbeat refresh (:399); writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive (:288-302) fsyncs at :294. Crash right after the reclaim rename can lose the reclaimed lease. Bounded P2: renameSync atomic (no torn lease), total loss reopens a clean no-owner state re-acquirable at :369, re-read :383-384 serializes concurrent racers, heartbeat refresh every ttl/2=30s (:420-421) self-heals.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add fsyncSync (file fd + parent dir fd) to writeOwnerLeaseFile's rename path for parity with the exclusive writer, OR document that reclaim durability is intentionally weaker because the lease self-heals via heartbeat + exclusive re-acquire.
findingDetails.scopeProof: both writers in-range (3419e0a3e9); fsync present :294, absent :281-285; reclaim caller :382, heartbeat caller :399; working file == reviewed HEAD blob.
findingDetails.claim: writeOwnerLeaseFile (reclaim + heartbeat) renames without fsync unlike writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive (:294); a crash right after rename can lose the reclaimed lease.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether the lost-reclaim window is exploitable into a double-owner: it is not (atomic rename, clean-no-owner loss mode, re-read serialization :383-384, 30s heartbeat self-heal). Impact bounded to a transient lost reclaim -> advisory.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Reclaim durability intentionally weaker than fresh-acquire because the lease is short-lived (30s heartbeat) and self-healing; exclusive writer fsyncs only because fresh-acquire CAS establishes single ownership. Plausible but undocumented -> remains a real low hardening gap.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: N/A (already P2); drops to non-finding only if an inline comment documents the intentional asymmetry.

traceabilityChecks: working file byte-identical to reviewed HEAD 12de3d3a7e (git diff empty, exit 0)
F-002 classification ordering added by in-range 3419e0a3e9; EPERM branch in in-range changelog-006-009
canonical IDs F-002/F-004 reconciled against strategy.md:42 (iteration-002 labeled F-004 as bullet '3')
integrationEvidence: changelog-006-009-launcher-eperm-parity-fix.md: EPERM->live-lease is RCA-confirmed cross-sandbox-parity design intent (downgrades F-002)
launcher-lease.vitest.ts:360-367: reclaim test covers ESRCH dead-pid only; no EPERM/classifyOwnerLease coverage (EPERM-mock vitest is a documented follow-up)
edgeCases: dispatch framing 'EPERM mishandled relative to heartbeat start' refined: startOwnerLeaseHeartbeat (:1215) runs only post-acquisition-win; EPERM-hitting launchers return {acquired:false} at :361 and bridge/exit before any heartbeat -> no heartbeat-start race, real defect is the :339<:348 reachability gap
NEW adjacent observation (no standalone finding): classifyOwnerLease(holder) at :374/:388 on a losing race can label EPERM diagnostically -> log+bridge-target only, no correctness impact
F-004 self-heal bounded: lost reclaim recovers within one 30s heartbeat or next exclusive acquire
ruledOut: F-002 as a P1 must-fix gate bug (downgraded P2): only stuck trace needs cross-uid PID reuse, contradicting documented same-user multi-sandbox deployment; common EPERM case correct-by-design
'EPERM mishandled relative to heartbeat start' as a distinct race: heartbeat start is post-acquisition-win only, never reached on EPERM
F-004 escalation to P1 (double-owner via lost reclaim): atomic rename + clean-no-owner loss + re-read serialization + 30s heartbeat self-heal bound impact to transient
coverage: A1-verify complete: full acquire/classify/reclaim/heartbeat/respawn sequence read; both carried-forward A1 findings adjudicated with file:line + design-intent evidence; verdicts settled (F-002 P1->P2, F-004 P2 confirmed)
